title: START_SERVERS
text: START_SERVERS Start a number of servers under normal control by the Uniface Router. operation START_SERVERS params numeric Totalservers : IN ; (I4) string User : IN ; (C40) string Password : IN ; (C40) string UST : IN ; (C20) string Qualifiers : IN ; (C256) endparams Parameters Totalservers —number of instances of this server to be started. The Uniface Router will not exceed its $MAX_SERVERS limit. User —user name under which the servers will run. Password —password for the host machine. The user and password must be a valid combination on the Uniface Router’s host machine. UST —Uniface Server Type (UST) of the servers. Qualifiers —optional command line switches to use in starting the servers. Return Values If all servers were successfully started, $status is set to zero. Be aware that START_SERVERS might return an error even though some of the servers were started. Otherwise, see Return Codes . Use Allowed in all Uniface component types. Description The privileged-user operation START_SERVERS starts a number of servers under normal Uniface Router control. Restrictions The START_SERVERS operation is restricted to privileged users. For more information, see ADMLOGON . Observe the following restrictions for the UST value and the command line switches: If the UST is not specified, the default UST definition will be used, if one exists. If the switches you specify conflict with the switches specified in the UST’s definition in the Uniface Router’s assignment file, the values in the assignment file take precedence. If the UST you enter matches a specific or a default UST definition, you may not use the /dir and /asn switches. Otherwise, these switches and the switches /dnp , /ex , /shr , /su , -nosu , /pri , and /drv may be used. You may not specify the UST using the /ust switch. You may not use the /max , /maxidle , and /maxreq switches. The /drv switch determines the type of server to be started, for example, ORA for an Oracle database server, FIL for a file server, or S3C for a Uniface Application Server. If the /drv switch is not specified, it defaults to the ANY server. For a database server, you may not specify the database user name and password; they will be determined by the first client request assigned to the server. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
